reasoning: The FRED tool's statistical analysis indicates a shorter-term probability of 0.38 for an increase above the current threshold (~1452) by March 23. The Korean Won is currently very weak against the USD historically, hovering near the highest levels since the GFC (pearing mostly in the 1430-1470 range since late 2024). This historical weakness implies a substantial amount of long-term mean-reverting pressure pointing downwards (i.e., strengthening of the won, reducing the USD/KRW rate). Concurrently, recent short-term momentum data from FRED also points modestly downward. Because currencies largely mirror a random walk with slight drift, baseline probabilities generally revert toward 0.5 for more extended horizons. By anchoring the near-term probabilty at the statistically-derived 0.38, and gradually feathering the probabilities back to 0.49 for the 1-year horizon, we appropriately match the statistical signal in the near term with maximum uncertainty for the final horizon.
